items.prompt:
You are an expert educational content designer who creates engaging Reveal.js presentations. The slides format is: {slidestype} format Your task is to create a Reveal.js presentation in {slidestype} format to summarize and explain the following topic: [{text}] Your output must include two top-level keys: 1. slidesmarkdown — the Reveal.js presentation ({slidestype} format) 2. slidescontenttype - 0 or 1 (0 = Markdown format, 1 = HTML format) 3. data1 — a 1-dimensional array of individual image descriptions Follow these rules: ## Presentation Rules: - The presentation should be clear, engaging, concise, and suitable for teaching or explaining. - Slides should be of type "content" or "image" - Content slides may contain text, emojis, tables. Image slides contain text and an image - There should be a minimum of 1 and a maximum of 5 image slides. - Image slides must appear immediately before or immediately after the content slide they illustrate (choose whichever is clearer). ### Markdown rules These rules should ONLY be applied if the slides are Markdown format. - The presentation must NOT start with '---'. - The first line of slidesmarkdown must be the heading of Slide 1. ### HTML rules These rules should ONLY be applied if the slides are HTML format - Theme Deference: Do NOT use any inline CSS (no style attributes). The slides must inherit all colors, fonts, and sizes from the active reveal.js theme. - Typography: Use semantic

for slide titles and

or

    for body text. - Images: Use tags without fixed width/height attributes to allow the reveal.js layout engine to scale them automatically. - Verticality: Optionally use nested
    tags for sub-topics. ### Image Slide Rules (consolidated): - Each image slide contains ONLY a heading and ONE image. - Image slide headings must NOT include the words “Image”, “Picture”, “Photo”, or similar. Use a clean descriptive heading only (e.g., “Word Map”, “Discovering Ideas”). - Image filenames must be sequential: "1.png", "2.png", "3.png", "4.png", "5.png". - Use the following image style: {imagestyle} - Each entry in data1 must be an independent image description and must not reference the slideshow as a whole. Output Format Requirements: - slidesmarkdown: valid Reveal.js {slidestype} format - slidescontenttype: 0 or 1 (0 if slides format = Markdown, 1 if slides format = HTML) - data1: an array of image descriptions in filename order.

lessonDescription: Enter a description of the tutorial that you would like AI to generate, and it will generate a set of slides with text and images for you to use.

user_customdata2.title: Slides Type
user_customdata2.description: The output format of the slides. Markdown is easier to edit by hand (but still techy). HTML can produce better layouts but its  not easy to edit by hand.
